detaileddescription.para: Pulse information in the labnotebook is stored as PX_RY:.... This regular expression can be used to match the prefix in order to remove it with RemovePrefix.

briefdescription.para: Store the newly calculated rerun trials in the labnotebook and return the minimum and maximum of it.
Return a 2D wave with the headstage QC result as a function of the set sweep count.

detaileddescription.para: This function does return the result for all active headstages. The headstage argument is needed for fetching all sweeps from the current SCI.

briefdescription.para: Return the stimulus set sweep count for the given sweep.
Return pass/fail state of the set.

detaileddescription.para: This is true iff we have for very stimulus set sweep count a passing headstage

briefdescription.para: Return pass/fail state of the sweep.

detaileddescription.para: This is true iff we have for the current stimulus set sweep count a passing headstage

briefdescription.para: Return 1 if we are currently acquiring the last sweep in the stimulus set, 0 otherwise.
Given a list of pulses by their indizes, this function return only the diagonal ones which are matching the given sweep.

memberdef.argsstring: (WaveText inputLBN, variable pulseIndex, variable region, variable headstage, WaveOrNull data=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: SC_AddPulseRegionLBNEntries

briefdescription.para: Add a new PX_PY:.. entry in to the wave inputLBN

detaileddescription.para: The value part after the : is only present if data is passed in

briefdescription.para: Return the PA plot waves from the databrowser connected to the given device.
Return the diagonal pulses for the given sweep.
Return the spike numbers and positions in waves prepared for labnotebook writing.

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, variable sweepNo, variable minimumSpikePosition, variable idealNumberOfSpikes)
memberdef.name: SC_ProcessPulses

briefdescription.para: Fetch the pulses from the PA plot and write the results into the labnotebooks.
Convert a numeric spike counts state to its string.
Determine the spike counts state.

detaileddescription.para:
We assume:
minimum < maximum
idealNumberOfSpikes >= 1
All numbers are integers

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, wave minimum, wave maximum, variable idealNumberOfSpikes)
memberdef.name: SC_SpikeCountsCalc

briefdescription.para: Determine the spike counts state for all headstages.
Check that the we have found the expected number of spikes per pulse.

simplesect.para: Spike counts state according to SpikeCountsStateConstants stringified

briefdescription.para: Calculate the QC state of a list of spike positions from one pulse.

detaileddescription.para: All are in Pulse active coordinate system

briefdescription.para: Calculate the QC state of the spike positions of each pulse.

detaileddescription.para: All values are in PA crd, see SC_SpikeControl().

parameterdescription.para: spike position of each pulse, ordered per headstage, for the current sweep

briefdescription.para: Replace all points inside an oodDAQ region with NaN in the data wave.
Return a wave ready for the labnotebook with the spontaneous spike check QC entries.
Determine the headstage QC result.

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, variable sweepNo, wave spikeNumbersLBN, WaveOrNull spikePositionsLBN, variable minimumSpikePosition, variable idealNumberOfSpikes)
memberdef.name: SC_DetermineQCState

briefdescription.para: Determine and write the QC states to the labnotebook.
Check if we can still skip sweeps without running more than maxTrials
Check if we have exhausted the available trials on all headstages.
Perform various actions on QC failures.
Analysis function to check sweeps for failed pulses and rerun them if the pulses failed.

detaileddescription.para: For judging the quality of the spike positions we have introduced a new coordinate system which we call pulse active coordinate system (PA crd).
Consider the following pulse on the DA wave and the response spike in the AD wave

para.verbatim:
and the let pulse active start at 5ms and end at 15ms. We define the start as 0 and the end as 100. Therefore the spike position will be 110 in `PA crd` which is one point after the end of the pulse.
The graphs were made with http://asciiflow.com.

briefdescription.para: SC Spike Control analysis function for multi patch sequence
